user_query:
Run a non-invasive security and system health audit of our OpenClaw host and produce two artifacts:

1) Write a clear narrative report to output/healthcheck/system_health_report.md that includes:
- A System Health Score (0–100) at the top
- An Executive Summary in plain language
- Sections titled exactly: Security Audit Findings, Update Status, Firewall Status, Open Ports, System Vitals
- Bullet-pointed, actionable recommendations
- Every finding should include a severity tag in square brackets, e.g., [low], [medium], [high], or [critical]

2) Write a machine-readable summary to output/healthcheck/system_health_summary.json with this shape:
{
  "systemHealthScore": number (0–100),
  "auditFindings": [ { "id": string, "title": string, "severity": "low"|"medium"|"high"|"critical", "category": string } ],
  "updates": { "outdated": boolean, "outdatedCount": number },
  "firewall": { "active": boolean, "tool": string },
  "openPorts": number[],
  "os": { "name": string, "version": string },
  "generatedAt": string (ISO 8601 timestamp)
}

Do not make any changes to the system as part of this audit; report only. If any data isn’t discoverable, state the assumption in the report and reflect "unknown" appropriately (e.g., firewall.tool = "unknown"). Also include 2–3 sentences on how you calculated the System Health Score in the report.
